prerequisites: A running Chroma database, either in client/server mode (reachable via host and port) or persistent mode (an absolute local path prefixed with /local).
A Chroma collection name to write records into (configured via the indexing.collection_name field).

An embedding strategy chosen for the embedding field: a managed provider (OpenAI, Cohere, or Azure OpenAI), an OpenAI-compatible endpoint, embeddings supplied in the source records, or Chroma's default embedding function.
If using a managed embedding provider, an account and API key with that provider: an OpenAI API key for OpenAI, a Cohere API key for Cohere, or an Azure endpoint URL plus API key for Azure OpenAI.
Processing settings defined under the processing field: the text fields to embed and the metadata fields to store, plus the text chunk size used for splitting.

authMethods.name: Chroma client/server credentials (username and password)
authMethods.summary: When connecting to a Chroma server in client/server mode, configure the host and port and supply a username and password under indexing.auth_method. The password is stored in the indexing.auth_method.password secret field. Username and password are optional and only needed if the Chroma instance enforces basic authentication; persistent (local path) mode requires no credentials.

authMethods.name: OpenAI embedding API key
authMethods.summary: Selecting OpenAI as the embedding provider requires an OpenAI API key, stored in the embedding.openai_key secret field. The connector uses it to call OpenAI's embeddings API to vectorize the configured text fields.

authMethods.name: Cohere embedding API key
authMethods.summary: Selecting Cohere as the embedding provider requires a Cohere API key, stored in the embedding.cohere_key secret field, used to generate embeddings via the Cohere API.

authMethods.name: Azure OpenAI embedding API key
authMethods.summary: Selecting the Azure OpenAI embedding option requires your Azure endpoint URL and an API key. The key is stored in the embedding.api_key secret field and is used against your Azure-hosted OpenAI deployment to produce embeddings.

setupSteps.title: Prepare your Chroma instance
setupSteps.body: Stand up a Chroma database in either client/server mode (note its host and port) or persistent mode (note the absolute local path, prefixed with /local). Decide on the collection name the connector will write to.

setupSteps.title: Configure the indexing connection
setupSteps.body: Set the indexing field with your collection_name and choose the auth_method. For client/server mode, provide the host and port; if the server enforces authentication, also provide the username and the password (stored in indexing.auth_method.password). For persistent mode, provide the local path instead.

setupSteps.title: Choose and configure the embedding provider
setupSteps.body: Set the embedding field to your chosen strategy. For OpenAI, supply the OpenAI API key (embedding.openai_key). For Cohere, supply the Cohere API key (embedding.cohere_key). For Azure OpenAI, supply the Azure endpoint and API key (embedding.api_key). Alternatively use an OpenAI-compatible endpoint, embeddings carried in source record fields, or Chroma's default embedding function, none of which require a managed provider key.

setupSteps.title: Define processing and metadata behavior
setupSteps.body: Under the processing field, specify which text fields to embed and which fields to store as metadata, and set the chunk size used to split text. Optionally enable omit_raw_text to exclude the original document text from what is stored in Chroma.

setupSteps.title: Test the connection
setupSteps.body: Run the connector's connection check to confirm it can reach the Chroma instance, authenticate (if credentials are configured), access the target collection, and reach the selected embedding provider before starting a sync.

sources.title: Pinecone Connector | Airbyte Documentation (shared vector embedding spec: OpenAI, Cohere, Azure OpenAI)
